Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: TurboQuant-WASM – Google's vector quantization in the browser 145 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. Ruckus: Racket for iOS 107 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. OpenScreen is an open-source alternative to Screen Studio 166 upvotes, 27 comments
  4. Show HN: I made open source, zero power PCB hackathon badges 67 upvotes, 8 comments
  5. Show HN: A game where you build a GPU 580 upvotes, 144 comments
  6. The Indie Internet Index – submit your favorite sites 115 upvotes, 24 comments
  7. Show HN: M. C. Escher spiral in WebGL inspired by 3Blue1Brown 42 upvotes, 6 comments
  8. Training mRNA Language Models Across 25 Species for $165 135 upvotes, 30 comments
  9. Breaking Enigma with Index of Coincidence on a Commodore 64 32 upvotes, 4 comments
  10. AWS Engineer Reports PostgreSQL Perf Halved by Linux 7.0, Fix May Not Be Easy 171 upvotes, 42 comments
  11. Embarrassingly simple self-distillation improves code generation 576 upvotes, 169 comments
  12. Some Unusual Trees 265 upvotes, 76 comments
  13. LLM Wiki – example of an "idea file" 112 upvotes, 28 comments
  14. Introduction to Computer Music [pdf] 82 upvotes, 21 comments
  15. Show HN: Contrapunk – Real-time counterpoint harmony from guitar input, in Rust 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. Advice to Young People, the Lies I Tell Myself (2024) 71 upvotes, 18 comments
  17. Components of a Coding Agent 195 upvotes, 66 comments
  18. AI that copied musical artist files copyright claim against that artist 28 upvotes, 6 comments
  19. Apple approves driver that lets Nvidia eGPUs work with Arm Macs 397 upvotes, 174 comments
  20. How many products does Microsoft have named 'Copilot'? 496 upvotes, 246 comments
  21. Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens 137 upvotes, 69 comments
  22. Isseven 74 upvotes, 37 comments
  23. Rubysyn: Clarifying Ruby's Syntax and Semantics 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. The CMS is dead, long live the CMS 130 upvotes, 77 comments
  25. Functional programming accellerates agentic feature development 34 upvotes, 18 comments
  26. A case study in testing with 100+ Claude agents in parallel 36 upvotes, 20 comments
  27. Writing Lisp Is AI Resistant and I'm Sad 43 upvotes, 30 comments
  28. Shooting down ideas is not a skill 123 upvotes, 120 comments
  29. Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model 162 upvotes, 166 comments
  30. VR Realizes the Cyberspace Metaphor 8 upvotes, 4 comments
